title: Friday Tempo Durability
objective: Hold one controlled sub-threshold stimulus during a high-stress week: three tempo blocks plus steady endurance keep aerobic durability and rhythm without the recovery cost of threshold work.
why_now: Work stress is high and acute wellness has turned (RHR 53->58, HRV 36->32, sleep 8.6h->6.7h over two days). Form reads green (+12) only because training load is low, so this is life stress, not training readiness - the wrong week to spend it on an FTP read. Tempo gives the requested intervals while staying clearly below FTP so it absorbs cleanly.

source_context.week_objective: Absorb a high-stress week: keep frequency, cut intensity

targets.notes: Tempo, not threshold. Blocks sit at 83-88% FTP (216-229 W), RPE 5-6 - conversational-hard, never a grind. This is a deliberately easy-intensity week; if the legs or HR don't want it, downgrade rather than force it.

warmup.notes: Easy to high-Z2 ramp. Let the legs and HR come around before the first block; no rush on a tired week.

steps.notes: Tempo block - 216-229 W. Smooth, steady, breathing controlled and able to speak a short sentence. Stay off the top of the range; this should feel sustainable, not threshold.
Easy spin between blocks. Soft pedal, let HR settle.

cooldown.notes: Steady Z2 to finish - durability volume, fuel and drink, comfortable cadence. This is the aerobic part of the day; keep it relaxed.

nutrition.pre: Normal carb-forward meal 2-3h before, ~1g carbs/kg. Nothing special - it's a moderate day.
nutrition.during: 30-50g carbs/hour and drink to thirst; on a low-sleep week, fueling well also protects mood and the rest of your day.
nutrition.post: 20-30g protein plus carbs within an hour or just a normal balanced meal if you eat soon after.
nutrition.supplements: None needed. Skip the pre-workout caffeine if it would touch tonight's sleep - sleep is the priority this week.

recovery.post_session: Refuel, hydrate, gentle left-calf roll if it feels tight.
recovery.evening: Guard sleep hard tonight - earlier wind-down, screens down. Sleep is the real training input this week.
recovery.next_morning_check: Note legs, sleep, HRV/resting HR before the weekend ride; if RHR is still climbing, downgrade Saturday to easy.

skill_focus.action: Hold each tempo block at even, relaxed power - no surging, no chasing the top of the range.
skill_focus.why: Steady sub-threshold output builds durability without the fatigue spike that would compound a stressed week.

skill_focus.action: Keep shoulders, hands and jaw relaxed; breathe in rhythm.
skill_focus.why: On a low-sleep day, wasted tension inflates HR and RPE and makes an easy effort feel hard.

mental_focus.intention: Take the stimulus, not the strain. Easy week, on purpose.
mental_focus.why: Channelling stress into a hard ride feels good for an hour and costs you for three days right now. Tempo keeps the rhythm and lets the body recover.
mental_focus.reset: If you feel yourself pushing the pace up, exhale, drop back into the range, and remind yourself this is a recovery-protecting week.

pre_interval_cues: This is tempo, not threshold - cap the effort.
If the legs feel flat in the warmup, plan to downgrade.
during_workout_cues: Able to speak a short sentence.
Off the top of the range.
execution_notes.if_too_easy: Good - leave it easy. At most hold the top of the range (229 W). Do not turn tempo blocks into threshold; the point of the week is to absorb, not to push.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: Drop to 2 blocks, or convert the whole main set to straight Z2 endurance. A flat HR/legs day means the body is using its capacity on life stress - back off without guilt.
execution_notes.success_criteria: Completed the tempo blocks at RPE <=6 feeling sustainable, finished the ride relaxed rather than emptied. If you finished wanting more, that is exactly right for this week.
post_workout_capture: Did tempo feel sustainable or like a grind
